purpose: Isolate whether same-condition checkpoint restart (segmenting) perturbs tau_100_max/tau_mean_max, independent of fidelity. 17.5 RPM, fidelity=9 (matches existing L9 single-shot baseline run_id=44133566), 4 segments mirroring L10 17.5 RPM chain structure exactly (t_end/t_checkpoint copied from L10 template run_ids 61d53bc5/7c00faee/1986b360/80c3f213), omega_b_prev never set (same-condition restart only, no cross-condition warm-start).

results.invalidation_reason: baseline_l9_single_shot_run_id (44133566) was discovered, after this experiment was reported as complete, to have been overwritten by an abandoned cross-condition warm-start pilot from 22.5 RPM (params.json shows t_checkpoint=18.85, omega_b_prev=2.356194) -- not a genuine cold-start single-shot run. The 'baseline_single_shot' values above are that warm-started run's outputs, not a clean reference. The comparison and its conclusion do not hold. Redone on 30.0 RPM in ../l9_l10_checkpoint_isolation_test_30rpm/, whose baseline was verified clean (t_checkpoint=None, omega_b_prev=None, raw data starting at t=0) before use.
results.note: This entire run_ids/segments structure itself is fine -- it's the comparison target that was wrong, not the segmented chain built here. Kept for the record rather than deleted.
